##NAMES=Marsturite
##RRUFFID=R141119
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=NaCaMn^2+^_3_Si_5_O_14_(OH)
##LOCALITY=Gozaisho mine, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Marcus Origlieri
##DESCRIPTION=Massive aggregate of parallel prismatic or bladed yellowish orange crystals
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R141119
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_0.89_Ca_0.11_)Ca_1.00_(Mn^2+^_2.85_Ca_0.12_Mg_0.03_Fe^2+^_0.01_)(Si_4.95_V^3+^_0.02_)O_14_(OH)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 7.671 b: 11.692 c: 6.751 alpha: 92.31 beta: 94.39 gamma: 106.92 volume: 576.3 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
